title: uputregs
text: uputregs Copy a string value to the specified general variable ($1 ... $99). Explain this code long uputregs (shortregNbr, unsigned char*text); Copy Parameters regNbr Number of the Uniface general variable. This must be from 1 to 99. text Text to put into the general variable. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uputregs Value Meaning -1 Some error occurred 0 An empty string was assigned — All other values indicate success Use Library: yrtl Description uputregs copies the string text to the Uniface general variable (also known as dollar register) with the specified number regNbr , which can range from 1 to 99. There is no check on the validity of this number; if it is out of range, a crash is likely to result. If you want the string to consist of multiple lines (to be displayed as such in a unifield), use carriage return characters (CR, ASCII 13) as line separators, as shown in the example below. Except for CR, you should not be using any other non-printable characters (ASCII values lower than 32 or greater than 127) because these might be specifically interpreted by Uniface. If text is zero, or an empty string, the general variable will be assigned the value NULL, and the function will return zero. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void putlines () { (void)uputregs(1, "This is\ra string of\rthree lines"); } Copy Related Links ugetregs Get the string value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). uputreg Copy a long integer value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99). uputregf Copy a double-precision floating-point value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99).
